D1 Optics L1 Identify Atom

Transverse vs Longitudinal Waves

compare a wiggling string and a compressing spring side by side

Particles in a transverse wave move perpendicular to its travel; in a longitudinal wave they move along it. Light is the former, sound the latter.

Slide the frequency. The upper string beads displace vertically (transverse); the lower spring beads bunch and stretch horizontally (longitudinal). Both waves travel to the right.
